parentQuestion: The parasympathetic division innervates more organs than the sympathetic division.

scaffolds.q: Both ANS divisions innervate the heart, lungs, and GI tract.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Most major visceral organs receive DUAL innervation from both sympathetic and parasympathetic divisions.

scaffolds.q: Blood vessels throughout the body need nervous system control for pressure regulation.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Blood vessel diameter determines blood pressure. The ANS controls vascular smooth muscle.

scaffolds.q: Most blood vessels are innervated by the sympathetic division ONLY.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The parasympathetic generally doesn't innervate blood vessels (except in erectile tissue). Vascular tone is controlled by sympathetic activity alone.

scaffolds.q: Sweat glands are innervated by the sympathetic division ONLY.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Sweating for thermoregulation is sympathetic (though the neurotransmitter is ACh, not norepinephrine).

scaffolds.q: The adrenal medulla receives sympathetic input ONLY.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Sympathetic preganglionic neurons synapse directly on adrenal chromaffin cells. No parasympathetic involvement.

scaffolds.q: Arrector pili muscles (goosebumps) are sympathetic ONLY.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! These tiny muscles that raise hair follicles receive only sympathetic innervation.

scaffolds.q: The sympathetic reaches MORE structures (blood vessels, sweat glands, adrenal) than parasympathetic.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The sympathetic innervates everything the parasympathetic does, PLUS blood vessels, sweat glands, adrenal medulla, and arrector pili.

scaffolds.q: The parasympathetic does NOT innervate more organs than the sympathetic.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The opposite is true - the sympathetic has BROADER distribution than the parasympathetic.

scaffolds.q: The statement is FALSE - sympathetic actually innervates MORE structures.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is FALSE. The parasympathetic is limited to visceral organs via cranial and sacral outflow. The sympathetic reaches all of that PLUS blood vessels, sweat glands, and more.
